pageName: What Is TiDB? Open-Source Distributed SQL Database

meta.title: What Is TiDB? Open-Source Distributed SQL Database
meta.description: Learn what TiDB is: an open-source distributed SQL database supporting HTAP workloads, MySQL compatibility, and horizontal scalability without sharding.

props.headline: What Is TiDB?
props.subheadline: An open-source, distributed SQL database that supports Hybrid Transactional and Analytical Processing (HTAP) workloads. Fully MySQL compatible. Scales horizontally without manual sharding.

props.title: How TiDB Works
props.subtitle: TiDB separates compute from storage, allowing each layer to scale independently based on your workload.

items.title: TiDB Server
items.description: The stateless SQL layer that parses queries, generates execution plans, and connects to storage. Add more nodes to handle more concurrent queries.

items.title: TiKV Storage Engine
items.description: Row-based distributed storage optimized for transactional (OLTP) workloads. Automatically replicates data across nodes for high availability.

items.title: TiFlash Columnar Replica
items.description: Columnar storage optimized for analytical (OLAP) queries. Stays synchronized with TiKV in real time for seamless HTAP.

items.title: PD Placement Driver
items.description: The cluster brain handling scheduling, metadata management, and load balancing across all nodes.

items.title: MySQL Compatibility
items.description: TiDB speaks the MySQL wire protocol. Your existing applications, ORMs, and MySQL-compatible tools work out of the box with zero code changes.

items.title: Horizontal Scalability
items.description: Add nodes to grow capacity. TiDB scales compute and storage independently, eliminating manual sharding and application-layer partitioning logic.

items.title: HTAP on One Platform
items.description: TiKV handles transactions. TiFlash handles analytics. Both operate on the same live data in real time. No ETL, no delays, no separate warehouse.

items.title: Cloud-Native Design
items.description: Runs natively on Kubernetes via TiDB Operator. Deploy on any major cloud or use TiDB Cloud for fully managed, serverless experience.

items.title: Strong Consistency
items.description: ACID transactions across distributed nodes with automatic failover and multi-replica replication ensure zero data loss and high availability.

items.title: Open Source
items.description: Released under Apache 2.0 license. TiKV is a CNCF Graduated project—one of the most rigorously vetted cloud-native projects available.

props.title: Built for Modern Workloads
props.subtitle: TiDB powers financial services, SaaS platforms, real-time analytics, and AI applications.

items.title: Financial Services
items.description: Strict consistency, zero data loss, and high availability across regions. Perfect for payment processing, ledgers, and core banking.

items.title: SaaS & Internet Scale
items.description: Scale storage and compute independently as user base grows. No re-architecting, no hand-rolled sharding strategies.

items.title: Real-Time Analytics
items.description: Run analytical queries directly against live transactional data. No nightly ETL, no stale dashboards, no separate warehouse.

items.title: AI Applications
items.description: Native vector search for RAG pipelines and agent memory systems. Combine structured data with semantic search in one database.

tabs.label: Scaling
tabs.description: Traditional single-node MySQL or PostgreSQL hit write bottlenecks. You add read replicas, then face sharding complexity.
bullets: TiDB scales horizontally by adding nodes
No manual sharding or application-layer routing logic

tabs.label: Analytics
tabs.description: Traditional setups require separate data warehouses, ETL pipelines, and stale copies of your data.
bullets: TiDB runs OLTP and OLAP on the same live dataset
No ETL jobs or sync delays

tabs.label: Operations
tabs.description: Managing MySQL at scale means complex sharding logic, replication challenges, and operational overhead.

items.a: TiDB is an open-source, distributed SQL database built for Hybrid Transactional and Analytical Processing (HTAP) workloads. It supports the MySQL protocol, scales horizontally across commodity hardware, and is maintained by PingCAP under the Apache 2.0 license.
Yes. TiDB implements the MySQL wire protocol, so most applications built on MySQL connect to TiDB without code changes. Standard MySQL clients, drivers, and ORMs are all supported.

Yes. TiDB is released under the Apache 2.0 license and is free to use, modify, and distribute. Its storage engine, TiKV, is a CNCF Graduated project—one of the most rigorously vetted tiers in the cloud-native ecosystem.

HTAP stands for Hybrid Transactional and Analytical Processing. It means TiDB can handle both OLTP (transactional) and OLAP (analytical) workloads on the same live dataset—eliminating the need for separate databases or ETL pipelines between them.

TiDB scales horizontally by adding nodes. Compute (TiDB Server) and storage (TiKV, TiFlash) scale independently, so you can grow each layer based on your actual bottleneck without over-provisioning or re-architecting your application.

items.q: What are the main components of TiDB?
items.a: TiDB has four main components: TiDB Server (stateless SQL layer), TiKV (row-based transactional storage), TiFlash (columnar analytical storage), and PD (Placement Driver for cluster management).

items.q: Can I run TiDB on Kubernetes?
items.a: Yes. TiDB runs natively on Kubernetes via TiDB Operator, making deployment straightforward on any major cloud provider. For a fully managed experience without ops overhead, use TiDB Cloud.

items.q: How do I get started with TiDB?
items.a: You have three options: TiDB Cloud (fully managed, includes free tier), TiDB Self-Managed (deploy on your own infrastructure), or TiDB Playground (try in your browser with no setup required).

props.title: Get Started with TiDB Today
props.subtitle: Choose the deployment model that fits your needs. All paths lead to the same powerful, scalable database.
